mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% across historical late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 3.0% signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces equity markets to discount future earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across a 5-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption financed by debt, which precedes a demand correction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a directional accuracy above 50% in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index during a post-shock recovery regime signals rapid supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during post-shock recovery periods do not yield positive 5-day returns more than 50% of the time, invalidating the supply normalization premium.
